comment: Information, structure, and relationships conveyed through presentation can be programmatically determined or are available in text.
Text is still visible and readable when resized to at least 200%.
Elements are accessible using a keyboard only.
There are no keyboard traps when navigating using a keyboard.
Focusable fields receive focus in an order that preserves meaning and operability.
Focus is visible when navigating using a keyboard.
When search receives focus, it does not initiate a change of context.
Selecting search does not change the context of the content without notifying the user.
Detailed labels or instructions are provided when a field requires user input.
If an error is detected then a suggestion(s) are provided to the user to correct.
For all elements the name and role can be programmatically determined.
Each input field has a label and an associated id
Text is visible/readable when the line spacing is at least 1.5 times the font size, letter spacing is at least 0.12 times the font size, and word spacing is at least 0.16 times the font size.
For fields that contain labels the name contains the text that is presented visually.
All status messages can be programmatically determined through role or properties such that they can be presented to the user by assistive technologies without receiving focus.
When a user interface component receives keyboard focus, the component is not entirely hidden due to author-created content.
